message:
fix(verifier_economy): tighten replay capsule freshness window validation to reject zero-length and future-issued windows

The replay_capsule_freshness_window_valid() function previously only checked
that the window duration was within bounds and the expiry hadn't passed. This
allowed two edge cases through:

1. Zero-length windows (issued_at == expires_at) — the old integer
   seconds comparison treated 0 as valid since it fell within 0..=MAX range
2. Future-issued capsules (issued_at > now) — a capsule that hasn't started
   its validity period yet was accepted as long as it hadn't expired

Fix: normalize timestamps to UTC before comparison, reject non-positive
durations explicitly (window <= Duration::zero()), and add an issued_at <= now
check so capsules cannot be pre-dated into the future.

New tests:
- test_capsule_integrity_rejects_zero_length_freshness_window
- test_capsule_integrity_rejects_future_issued_freshness_window
- zero_length_capsule_rejected_by_freshness_check (unit)
- future_issued_capsule_rejected_by_freshness_check (unit)
- Rename future_capsule_accepted → current_capsule_with_future_expiry_accepted
  to clarify what the test actually validates

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
